background: Table 3.4 lists fasting cholesterol values (mg/dl) for two groups of men.

task.query: Create a back-to-back stemplot of the fasting cholesterol values for the two groups of men using quintuple-split stem values.
task.answer:
Here is the back-to-back stemplot of the data using quintuple-split stem values:
\begin{gathered}
\text { Group } 1 \text { | |Group } 2 \\ \hline|1 \mathrm{t}| 3 \\
|1 \mathrm{f}| 45 \\
|1 \mathrm{~s}| 67 \\
98|1 .| 8889 \\
110|2 *| 011 \\
33332|2 \mathrm{t}| 22 \\
55544|2 \mathrm{f}| 4455 \\
76|2 \mathrm{~s}| 6 \\
9 \mid 2 . |\\
1|3 *| \\
2|3 \mathrm{t}| \\
|3 \mathrm{f}| 4 \\
( \times 100)
\end{gathered}

task.query: Describe the distribution of fasting cholesterol values for the two groups based on the stemplot.
task.answer: Notice that the distribution of group 1 is shifted down the axis toward the higher values on the stem showing it to have higher values on average.
